<<Course Structure and How to Get the Most Out of It>>

*highlighted section*  
**Understand the Course Structure First**  
A course is typically divided into modules, units, or lessons. Each section has specific goals, readings, and assessments. To get the most out of it, start by reviewing the syllabus and identifying key [topics], [deadlines], and [assignments]. Knowing the [structure] helps you plan your time effectively and align your efforts with [learning objectives].

*highlighted section*  
**Engage Actively with Content**  
Passive learning rarely leads to deep understanding. Actively participate by taking notes, asking [questions], and applying concepts through [exercises]. For example, if the course covers [math], try solving [problems] on your own before checking solutions. This builds [critical thinking] and [retention].

*highlighted section*  
**Use Available Resources**  
Most courses provide [resources] like videos, readings, and forums. Take advantage of [tutoring], [office hours], and [peer groups]. If you're stuck on a [concept], don’t hesitate to reach out to [instructors] or [mentors]. These interactions can clarify doubts and improve your [performance].

I think yes.
